business_glossary_term: Distribution Direct Store Delivery (DSD) Delivery ID
description: Unique identifier for the DSD delivery transaction. Primary key for the distribution_dsd_delivery product.
Reference to the DSD driver who executed this delivery. Links to workforce employee master data.
Reference to the retail store account where this delivery was executed. Links to the sales.retail_store master data.
Reference to the DSD route this delivery is part of. Links to the route master data defining the planned sequence of store visits.
The actual timestamp when the driver arrived at the retail store location. Used for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement.
The timestamp when this delivery record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery transaction.

Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the delivery attempt. Used for root cause analysis of delivery failures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store_closed|access_denied|incomplete_order|damaged_product|wrong_address|weather_delay|traffic_delay|vehicle_breakdown|none — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Free-text notes captured by the driver regarding delivery conditions, store feedback, or special circumstances encountered during the visit.
Business identifier for the DSD delivery transaction. Externally visible delivery document number used for tracking and proof of delivery reference.

Current lifecycle status of the DSD delivery transaction. Tracks progression from scheduled visit through completion or failure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_transit|arrived|in_progress|completed|failed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The total monetary value of products delivered during this visit, calculated at invoice price. Represents gross delivery value before returns and credits.
The end of the agreed delivery time window with the retail store. Part of Service Level Agreement (SLA) for OTIF measurement.
The beginning of the agreed delivery time window with the retail store. Part of Service Level Agreement (SLA) for OTIF measurement.
The timestamp when the driver departed from the retail store after completing delivery and merchandising activities.
Geographic latitude coordinate captured at delivery location for route verification and geospatial analytics.
Geographic longitude coordinate captured at delivery location for route verification and geospatial analytics.
Indicates whether the complete ordered quantity was delivered without shortages. Component of OTIF performance measurement.
Reference to the sales invoice document generated for this DSD delivery. Links to financial billing and accounts receivable.
The timestamp when this delivery record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.
The type of in-store merchandising activity performed during the delivery visit. Supports retail execution and On Shelf Availability (OSA) objectives.
The total time in minutes spent on merchandising activities during the store visit. Used for labor productivity analysis.
Indicates whether in-store merchandising activities (shelf stocking, rotation, display setup) were performed during this delivery visit.
The net monetary value of the delivery after subtracting return credits from gross delivery value. Represents actual secondary sales value.
Indicates whether the delivery arrived within the agreed delivery time window. Component of OTIF performance measurement.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether this delivery met On Time In Full (OTIF) performance criteria: arrived within delivery window and delivered complete order quantity.
The method used to capture proof of delivery from the store recipient. Electronic signature and photo are preferred for audit trail.
URL reference to the photo evidence of delivery captured at the store. Links to document management system for audit purposes.

business_glossary_term: Proof of Delivery (POD) Signature Image URL
description: URL reference to the stored electronic signature image captured as proof of delivery. Links to document management system.
The name of the store employee who received and acknowledged the delivery. Captured for proof of delivery and accountability.
The total monetary value of product returns credited to the store during this visit. Reduces net delivery value.
The planned arrival timestamp at the retail store based on route optimization and store delivery window requirements.
The unique identifier of this delivery record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Indicates whether temperature-sensitive products were maintained within required cold chain temperature ranges during delivery. Critical for food safety and quality compliance.
The total quantity of product cases delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) delivered.
The total quantity of product cases returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.
The total quantity of individual product units delivered to the store during this visit. Aggregate measure across all SKUs delivered.
The total quantity of individual product units returned from the store during this visit due to damage, expiration, or other quality issues.
Reference to the delivery vehicle used for this DSD delivery. Links to fleet asset master data.
The calendar date on which the DSD delivery visit to the retail store occurred. Business event date for reporting and analytics.

business_glossary_term: Distribution Direct Store Delivery (DSD) Route ID
description: Unique identifier for the DSD route record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: DSD route planning assigns a carrier to each route; the carrier_id FK enables carrier performance and cost analysis.
Identifier of the distribution center or depot from which this DSD route originates. Links to the facility master.
Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: DSD route aligns with a transport lane to calculate distance, fuel surcharge, and service level expectations.
Identifier of the driver or sales representative permanently or primarily assigned to this route. May be null for unassigned or pool routes.
Identifier of the sales or service territory to which this route is assigned. Used for sales force alignment and performance tracking.
Historical average order value per stop on this route in local currency. Used for revenue forecasting and route profitability analysis.
Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with this route (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date when this route configuration was discontinued or superseded. Null for currently active routes. Used for historical route analysis.
Date when this route configuration became active and operational. Used for historical route analysis and change tracking.
Estimated total driving time for the route in minutes, excluding stop service time. Used for driver shift planning and OTIF performance targets.
Estimated total time spent at customer stops for unloading, merchandising, and order processing in minutes. Combined with drive time for total route duration.
Timestamp when this route record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Date when this route was last reviewed and optimized for efficiency. Used to identify routes due for re-evaluation.
Boolean flag indicating whether this route includes merchandising activities (shelf stocking, display setup, POG compliance) in addition to delivery. True if merchandising is part of the service.
Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or route-specific considerations (e.g., 'Avoid highway during rush hour', 'Customer prefers morning delivery').
Total count of retail store or customer locations included in this route. Used for route capacity planning and driver workload estimation.
Target OSA percentage for stores on this route, representing the expected in-stock rate at point of sale. Used for DSD execution quality and replenishment effectiveness tracking.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Percentage
description: Target OTIF performance percentage for this route, representing the expected rate of deliveries completed on time and in full. Used for SLA management and driver performance evaluation.
Standard departure time from the DC for this route in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for driver scheduling and DC loading dock coordination.
Expected return time to the DC for this route in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Used for driver shift planning and vehicle turnaround scheduling.
Numeric priority ranking of this route relative to other routes in the territory or DC, with lower numbers indicating higher priority. Used for resource allocation during capacity constraints.
Boolean flag indicating whether this route requires temperature-controlled (refrigerated or frozen) transportation for product integrity. True if cold chain is required.
Boolean flag indicating whether the driver assigned to this route must hold hazmat certification due to regulated products (e.g., aerosols, flammables). True if certification is required.
Business identifier for the DSD route, used for operational reference and scheduling. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by logistics planning.
Human-readable name or description of the DSD route, often including geographic or customer identifiers (e.g., 'Downtown Metro Route A', 'North Region Convenience Stores').

Calculated efficiency score for this route based on distance, time, stops, and revenue, typically ranging from 0-100. Higher scores indicate more optimized routes. Used for continuous improvement initiatives.

Current operational status of the DSD route. Active routes are in regular service; inactive routes are permanently discontinued; suspended routes are temporarily paused; under_review routes are being optimized; seasonal_closed routes are off-season.

Classification of the DSD route based on service model: standard (regular scheduled delivery), express (same-day or expedited), bulk (high-volume customers), emergency (unplanned replenishment), seasonal (temporary routes for peak demand).
Comma-separated list of days of the week this route is scheduled to run (e.g., 'Mon,Wed,Fri' for a three-day-per-week route). Supports recurring schedule planning.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the route start point (typically the DC or depot). Used for route optimization and GPS tracking.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the route start point (typically the DC or depot). Used for route optimization and GPS tracking.
Total driving distance for the complete route in kilometers, from DC departure through all stops and return. Used for fuel cost estimation and vehicle range planning.
Minimum vehicle cargo capacity required in standard units (cases, pallets, or cubic meters) to fulfill typical route demand. Used for vehicle assignment.
Type of vehicle required to service this route based on cargo volume, weight, and product requirements (e.g., refrigerated for cold chain products).

business_glossary_term: Direct Store Delivery (DSD) Delivery Line ID
description: Unique identifier for the DSD delivery line item. Primary key for this product.
Reference to the parent DSD delivery header record. Links this line item to the overall store visit and delivery transaction.
Reference to the warehouse or distribution center location from which this product was sourced for delivery.
Reference to the DSD driver who delivered this line item. Used for driver performance tracking and accountability.
Reference to the trade promotion or promotional campaign associated with this delivery line, if applicable.
Foreign key linking to research.rd_project. Business justification: Tracks DSD pilot deliveries of new formulations, required for consumer test logistics and performance reporting.
Reference to the delivery route on which this line was delivered. Used for route optimization and logistics analysis.
Reference to the product master record for the SKU delivered or returned on this line.
Indicates whether cold chain temperature requirements were maintained throughout delivery. True if compliant, False if temperature excursion occurred.
Standard cost of goods sold for the delivered quantity. Used for margin analysis and profitability reporting.
Date and time when this delivery line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Actual quantity of the SKU delivered to the store during this visit. Used for secondary sales capture and inventory replenishment tracking.
Date and time when this specific line item was delivered or processed at the store. Used for OTIF performance tracking and delivery window compliance.
Total discount or promotional allowance applied to this line. Used for trade promotion settlement and margin analysis.
Product expiration or best-before date. Used for FEFO inventory rotation and shelf-life management in stores.
Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the product. Enables cross-system product identification and retail scanning.
Date and time when this delivery line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
Sequential line number within the delivery document. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items within the delivery.

Current status of this delivery line. Values: DELIVERED (fully delivered), PARTIAL (partially delivered), REJECTED (rejected by store), RETURNED (returned to warehouse), CANCELLED (cancelled before delivery).
Total monetary value for this delivery line (delivered quantity × unit selling price). Represents secondary sales revenue at store level.
Manufacturing lot or batch number for the delivered product. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management.
Date the product was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.
Net monetary value for this line after discounts and taxes (line_total_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount). Represents final revenue for this line.
Free-text notes or comments about this delivery line. May include special handling instructions, store feedback, or exception details.
Indicates whether the store was out of stock for this SKU at the time of delivery. True if OOS condition detected, False otherwise. Critical for OSA metrics.
Quantity of the SKU originally ordered by the store or planned for delivery. Used for fill-rate and OTIF analysis.
Indicates whether the product was placed according to the store planogram. True if compliant, False if deviation occurred.
Standardized code indicating why the delivery line was rejected by the store. Values: DAMAGED, WRONG_PRODUCT, QUALITY, UNAUTHORIZED, EXPIRED, OTHER.

Standardized code indicating why product was returned from the store. Values: DAMAGED (damaged goods), EXPIRED (past expiry date), OVERSTOCKED (excess inventory), QUALITY (quality issue), RECALL (product recall), UNSOLD (slow-moving), OTHER (other reason). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGED|EXPIRED|OVERSTOCKED|QUALITY|RECALL|UNSOLD|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Detailed free-text description of the return reason. Provides additional context beyond the standardized return reason code.
Quantity of the SKU returned from the store during this visit. Includes damaged goods, expired products, and unsold inventory.
Indicates whether the DSD driver confirmed proper shelf placement and merchandising of the delivered product. True if confirmed, False otherwise.
Total tax amount applicable to this delivery line. Calculated based on jurisdiction-specific tax rules.
Temperature control zone required for this product during storage and delivery. Values: AMBIENT (room temperature), CHILLED (refrigerated), FROZEN (frozen storage).

Unit of measure for quantities on this line. Common values: EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet), DZ (dozen), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), LT (liter), GL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|BX|PL|DZ|KG|LB|LT|GL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Selling price per unit for this SKU at the time of delivery. Used for revenue calculation and pricing analysis.
12-digit Universal Product Code for the product. Standard barcode identifier used in North American retail.
Reference to the delivery vehicle used to transport this line item. Used for fleet management and cold chain tracking.
Primary key for facility

Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_commitment. Business justification: Facilities have specific ESG commitments (energy, waste, emissions) that are tracked for performance dashboards and compliance.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the facility manager employee for each distribution center, required for operational performance metrics and compliance reporting.
Links DC to supply network node; distinct from manufacturing_facility which is a production site
Primary street address line for the distribution center facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
City or municipality where the distribution center is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
Date when the distribution center ceased operations. Null for active facilities. Used for historical analysis and network optimization studies.
Primary contact email address for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
Primary contact phone number for the distribution center. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
Three-letter ISO country code where the distribution center is located.
Timestamp when this distribution center record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Indicates whether the facility supports cross-docking operations where inbound goods are directly transferred to outbound shipments with minimal storage time.
Standard frequency in days for cycle counting inventory at this facility. Used for inventory accuracy management and audit compliance.
Business identifier code for the distribution center used across operational systems. Externally-known unique code for the facility.
Official business name of the distribution center or warehouse facility.
FK to manufacturing facility if co-located
FK to manufacturing facility
Number of dock doors designated for inbound receiving operations. Impacts receiving throughput capacity and scheduling.
Number of dock doors designated for outbound shipping operations. Impacts shipping throughput capacity and carrier scheduling.
Indicates whether this facility serves as a DSD hub for direct-to-retail delivery operations. DSD hubs bypass traditional distribution channels for faster store replenishment.
Purpose classification: distribution, cross-dock, hub

Classification of the distribution center based on temperature control and operational model. Ambient for room temperature, chilled for refrigerated, frozen for sub-zero, multi-temperature for mixed zones, DSD hub for Direct Store Delivery operations, cross-dock for flow-through distribution.
Indicates whether the facility is certified under FSC chain of custody standards for sustainable sourcing. Required for handling FSC-certified paper and wood-based products.
Indicates whether the facility is certified under Good Manufacturing Practice standards. Required for handling cosmetics, personal care, and certain consumer goods products.
Indicates whether the facility is certified to handle and store hazardous materials. Required for distribution of products with EPA or OSHA regulated substances.

Primary inventory rotation method used at this facility. FIFO (First In First Out) for standard goods, FEFO (First Expired First Out) for perishables and date-sensitive products, LIFO (Last In First Out) for specific use cases.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.
Expiration date of the facility lease agreement. Null for owned facilities. Used for lease renewal planning and facility strategy.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.
Timestamp when this distribution center record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Date when the distribution center commenced operations. Used for facility age analysis and depreciation calculations.
Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling inbound deliveries, outbound shipments, and workforce planning.
Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling weekend operations and carrier coordination.

Current operational state of the distribution center in its lifecycle. Active indicates full operations, inactive for temporarily closed, under construction for new facilities being built, decommissioned for permanently closed, seasonal for facilities operating only during peak periods, maintenance for temporary closure due to upgrades or repairs.
Target OSA performance percentage for stores served by this distribution center. OSA measures product availability at retail shelf level.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Percentage
description: Target OTIF performance percentage for this distribution center. OTIF measures the percentage of orders delivered on time and in full, a key supply chain KPI.
Classification of facility ownership model. Owned for company-owned facilities, leased for long-term leased properties, third-party logistics for 3PL-operated warehouses.
Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
SAP S/4HANA plant code representing this distribution center in the ERP system. Maps to the SAP MM and WM modules for material management and warehouse management.
SAP S/4HANA storage location code within the plant. Represents the primary storage location identifier for inventory transactions in SAP WM module.
Number of operational shifts per day at the facility. Used for labor planning, throughput capacity modeling, and operational cost analysis.
State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.
Maximum number of pallet positions available for storage. Key metric for inventory capacity planning and space utilization analysis.
Indicates whether the facility has temperature-controlled storage zones. True for facilities with chilled or frozen capabilities, false for ambient-only.
IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location. Used for scheduling, shift planning, and cross-facility coordination.
Total warehouse floor space capacity in square feet. Includes all storage, staging, and operational areas within the facility.
Unique site identifier in the Blue Yonder WMS system. Used for integration and synchronization between the lakehouse and the operational WMS platform.
Primary key for storage_location
Identifier of the parent distribution center facility where this storage location resides.
Velocity-based classification of the location for slotting optimization: A (high-velocity/fast-moving), B (medium-velocity), C (low-velocity/slow-moving).
Aisle designation within the warehouse layout for physical navigation and picking route optimization.
Bay or column position within the aisle for precise horizontal location reference.
Specific bin or slot position within the level for granular inventory placement and retrieval.
Date when the storage location was blocked or made unavailable for operations.
Explanation for why the location is blocked or unavailable, such as maintenance, damage, safety hold, or quality quarantine.
Timestamp when the storage location record was first created in the WMS system.
Scheduled frequency for cycle counting inventory at this location to maintain inventory accuracy and compliance.
Vertical level or shelf position within the bay for height-based slotting and equipment compatibility.
Indicates whether the location is designated for DSD operations where products bypass the DC and are delivered directly to retail stores.
Date when the storage location became active and available for warehouse operations.
Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage location for put-away and picking operations.
Date when the storage location is scheduled to be decommissioned or removed from active use. Null for indefinite locations.

business_glossary_term: First Expired First Out (FEFO) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the location supports FEFO inventory rotation logic for expiry-sensitive products (pharmaceuticals, food, cosmetics).

business_glossary_term: First In First Out (FIFO) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the location supports FIFO inventory rotation logic for age-based stock management.
Indicates whether the location is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials per OSHA and EPA regulations.
Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimeters for vertical clearance and equipment reach validation.
Date when the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location.
Timestamp when the storage location record was most recently updated in the WMS system.
Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.
Business identifier for the storage location as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Typically formatted as aisle-bay-level-bin (e.g., A01-B05-L03-P12).
Human-readable name or description of the storage location for operational reference.
Current operational status of the storage location indicating availability for inventory placement and retrieval.
Classification of the storage location by operational function: bulk storage, active pick face, reserve inventory, staging area, dock door, or cross-dock zone.
Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple lot or batch numbers for the same SKU or requires lot segregation.

business_glossary_term: Mixed Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) Allowed Flag
description: Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple SKUs simultaneously or requires single-SKU dedication.
Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face for order fulfillment operations.
Order fulfillment picking method supported by this location: batch picking, wave picking, zone picking, discrete (single-order), or cluster picking.
Algorithm used to assign incoming inventory to this location: directed (system-assigned), random, fixed (dedicated SKU), or dynamic (velocity-based).
Priority ranking for automated replenishment from reserve to pick locations, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.
Temperature control classification for the storage location to ensure product integrity and regulatory compliance (ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen <-18°C, controlled room temperature).
Maximum volume capacity of the storage location in cubic meters for space utilization and slotting algorithms.
Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms for safe load management and slotting optimization.
Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.
Logical zone classification within the distribution center for grouping locations by product category, velocity, or operational workflow (e.g., FAST-PICK, SLOW-MOVE, HAZMAT).
Unique identifier for the dock appointment record. Primary key for the dock appointment entity.
Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for this appointment. Links to carrier master data for performance tracking and compliance monitoring.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Allows Dock Scheduling System to capture which employee scheduled the appointment, enabling workload planning and audit of appointment creation.
Reference to the distribution center where this dock appointment is scheduled. Links to the facility managing the dock operations.

Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Receiving appointments are scheduled per purchase order, enabling the dock team to allocate doors and resources for each inbound PO.
Actual timestamp when the carrier truck checked in at the distribution center gate. Used to calculate on-time arrival performance and carrier compliance.
Actual number of cases handled during the appointment. Used for variance analysis and operational performance tracking.
Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations were completed and the truck departed. Used to calculate dock turnaround time and throughput metrics.
Actual number of pallets loaded or unloaded during the appointment. Compared against expected count to identify discrepancies.
Actual timestamp when loading or unloading operations began at the dock door. Marks the beginning of active dock utilization.
Actual total weight of goods handled in kilograms. Verified through weigh station or scale integration.
Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for scheduling and tracking. Externally visible identifier shared with carriers and suppliers.

Current lifecycle status of the dock appointment: scheduled (initial booking), confirmed (carrier acknowledged), checked_in (truck arrived at gate), in_progress (loading/unloading active), completed (finished), cancelled (appointment voided), no_show (carrier missed appointment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|confirmed|checked_in|in_progress|completed|cancelled|no_show — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the dock appointment purpose: inbound receipt (supplier delivery), outbound load (customer shipment), inter-DC transfer (internal movement), cross-dock (direct transfer), returns processing, or emergency delivery.
Advanced Shipping Notice identifier sent by supplier prior to shipment arrival. Used to pre-plan receiving operations and validate expected contents.
Free-text explanation for why the appointment was cancelled. Captures business context for cancelled or no-show appointments.
Timestamp when this dock appointment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for appointment booking lifecycle.
Indicates whether this is a cross-dock appointment where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound without storage. True for cross-dock operations.
Total time in minutes that the carrier was detained beyond the scheduled appointment window. Used to calculate detention charges and identify operational bottlenecks.
Physical dock door identifier assigned to this appointment (e.g., D-101, DOOR-A5). Indicates the specific loading/unloading bay at the distribution center.
Name of the driver assigned to this appointment. Used for gate check-in verification and security purposes.
Contact phone number for the driver. Used for real-time communication regarding delays, gate instructions, or operational issues.
Indicates whether this appointment is part of a Direct Store Delivery operation where goods bypass the DC and go directly to retail stores. True for DSD shipments.
Anticipated number of cases to be handled during this appointment. Supports detailed capacity and labor planning for case-level operations.
Anticipated number of pallets to be loaded or unloaded during this appointment. Used for dock capacity planning and labor scheduling.
Total expected weight of goods in kilograms for this appointment. Used for equipment planning and safety compliance.
UN hazard classification code for hazardous materials (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 8 for corrosives). Required for HAZMAT compliance and safety protocols.
Indicates whether this appointment involves hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and certified dock doors. True for HAZMAT shipments.
Timestamp when this dock appointment record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to appointment details or status.
Free-text notes and special instructions for this appointment. May include gate directions, special handling requirements, or operational alerts.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the appointment met On Time In Full performance criteria: arrived within scheduled window and delivered expected quantities. True if OTIF compliant.
Business priority classification for this appointment: standard (routine), high (expedited), urgent (same-day), or emergency (critical). Influences dock door assignment and resource allocation.
Sales order number associated with outbound load appointments. Links the dock appointment to customer orders being shipped.
Planned date for the carrier to arrive at the dock door. Used for day-level appointment planning and capacity management.
Precise timestamp when the appointment window closes. Carrier should complete loading/unloading by this time to maintain schedule compliance.
Precise timestamp when the appointment window begins. Carrier is expected to arrive at or after this time.
Indicates whether the security seal was intact upon arrival. True if seal is unbroken, false if compromised. Critical for security and quality control.
Security seal identifier applied to the trailer. Verified at check-in to ensure cargo integrity and prevent tampering.
Outbound shipment identifier for load appointments. References the shipment being loaded onto the carrier truck.
Indicates whether this appointment involves temperature-sensitive goods requiring cold chain management. True for refrigerated or frozen shipments.
Required temperature zone for goods in this appointment: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (-18°C), or deep frozen (below -25°C).
Unique identifier of the trailer or container arriving for this appointment. Used for yard management and asset tracking.
Primary key for inventory_position
Identifier of the distribution center where the inventory is physically located.

business_glossary_term: Storage Location Dc Location Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_storage_location. Business justification: Inventory position records on‑hand quantities at a specific DC location. Linking to distribution_storage_location provides the authoritative location details and removes the generic storage_location_i

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Supports Inventory Ownership Dashboard, assigning a responsible employee for each DC inventory position for accountability and cycle‑count oversight.
Identifier of the specific lot or batch of the SKU. Critical for traceability, quality control, and FEFO inventory rotation.
Identifier of the product SKU for which inventory position is tracked.
The actual measured weight of the inventory for catch weight items. Used for billing and compliance when item weight varies.
Indicates whether the SKU is a catch weight item requiring actual weight capture at transaction time (true) or standard weight (false).
The unit cost of the inventory lot. Used for inventory valuation and cost of goods sold calculations. Business confidential financial data.
The timestamp when this inventory position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for inventory valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The number of days the inventory has been on hand since receipt. Calculated as current date minus receipt date. Used for aging analysis.
The number of days remaining until the inventory lot expires. Calculated as expiry date minus current date. Critical for FEFO rotation and markdown decisions.
The date on which the product lot expires and can no longer be sold or distributed. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.
The physical condition classification of the inventory. Used for disposition decisions and channel restrictions.
The current operational status of the inventory position. Determines availability for picking and order fulfillment.
The date when this inventory position was last physically counted during a cycle count operation. Used to schedule next count and assess inventory accuracy.
The date when inventory was last moved into or out of this storage location. Used for slow-moving inventory identification.

The type of the last inventory movement transaction that affected this position (receipt, putaway, pick, replenishment, transfer, adjustment, return). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receipt|putaway|pick|replenishment|transfer|adjustment|return — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The timestamp when this inventory position record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness assessment.
The warehouse management system license plate number assigned to the handling unit. Used for tracking and automated material handling.
The alphanumeric lot or batch number assigned during manufacturing. Used for traceability and recall management.
The date on which the product lot was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO rotation.
Indicates the ownership model of the inventory (owned by company, consignment from supplier, customer-owned for returns, vendor-managed inventory).
The unique identifier of the pallet or handling unit on which the inventory is stored. Used for warehouse automation and tracking.
Indicates whether this inventory position is in a primary pick face location (true) or reserve storage (false). Pick face locations are optimized for order picking efficiency.
The date when the inventory was put away into this storage location after receiving. Used for aging analysis and FEFO compliance.
The quantity of on-hand inventory that has been allocated to outbound orders or reservations but not yet picked. Reduces available-to-pick quantity.
The quantity available for new order allocation and picking. Calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_allocated minus quantity_quarantine minus quantity_hold.
The quantity of inventory identified as damaged and not suitable for sale or distribution. Typically awaiting disposal or return to supplier.
The quantity of inventory placed on hold due to quality issues, customer complaints, or pending investigation. Not available for picking.
The total physical quantity of the SKU-lot currently present in the storage location. Measured in the SKU's base unit of measure.
The quantity of inventory placed in quarantine status pending quality inspection or regulatory clearance. Not available for picking.
The quantity of inventory reserved for specific customers, channels, or promotional programs. Subset of allocated quantity with additional business constraints.
The date when the inventory lot was received into the distribution center. Used for inventory aging and supplier performance tracking.
Indicates whether this inventory position requires replenishment from reserve to pick face (true) or not (false). Triggers automated replenishment tasks.
The timestamp when this inventory position snapshot was captured. Used for point-in-time inventory reporting and trend analysis.
The logical zone within the distribution center where the inventory is stored (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, hazmat, high-velocity, reserve).

Payment terms agreed with the customer for this order. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, COD (Cash on Delivery), or prepaid. Impacts accounts receivable and cash flow management.
Warehouse document number used to direct picking operations for this order. Generated by WMS when order is released to the warehouse floor.

Priority level assigned to the outbound order for warehouse sequencing and resource allocation. Critical orders receive highest priority, rush orders are expedited, expedited orders have faster processing, standard orders follow normal flow.
Date and time when delivery was confirmed by the recipient. Captured from carrier POD or customer signature. Used for OTIF measurement and freight audit.
Customer's purchase order number provided for order tracking and invoice reconciliation. Required for EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions with retail customers.
Date when the customer or system requests the order to be shipped from the distribution center. Used for warehouse planning and scheduling.
Date by which the order must be delivered to the customer destination. Critical for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement and SLA compliance.
Delivery speed commitment for the outbound order. Determines carrier selection, freight cost, and OTIF measurement criteria.

Transportation mode used for order delivery. Ground for truck, air for expedited freight, ocean for international, rail for bulk, parcel for small packages, LTL (Less Than Truckload) for partial loads, FTL (Full Truckload) for full loads. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|air|ocean|rail|parcel|ltl|ftl — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Free-text instructions for warehouse and logistics teams regarding special handling requirements. May include temperature control, fragile handling, hazmat procedures, or customer-specific delivery instructions.
Indicates whether the order requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation. True for cold chain products requiring refrigeration or freezing.
Total quantity of units across all line items in the outbound order. Used for warehouse capacity planning and OTIF fill rate calculation.
Total monetary value of the outbound order before taxes and freight charges. Used for order prioritization, credit limit checks, and revenue forecasting.
Total volume of the outbound order in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, truck cube utilization, and pallet configuration.
Total weight of the outbound order in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier capacity planning, and vehicle loading optimization.
Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and customer self-service tracking. Used for parcel and LTL shipments.
Identifier of the warehouse wave that includes this order. Wave picking groups orders for efficient batch picking and resource optimization.
Unique identifier for the outbound order line item.

Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_storage_location. Business justification: Outbound order line items are stored in a DC location before picking. Adding a FK to distribution_storage_location enables location lookup and normalizes location data.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Label version compliance check before order fulfillment guarantees the label used for the SKU is approved for the destination market.
Reference to the parent outbound fulfillment order header.

Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Attribution Report requires linking each order line to the promotion event that drove the sale; experts expect this FK to attribute revenue to promotions.

Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: PICKING: Outbound order line pick reduces the corresponding Stock Position quantity, linking order fulfillment to inventory balances.
Actual date the line was shipped from the distribution center.
Quantity allocated from warehouse inventory to this order line.
Quantity converted to base unit of measure for standardized reporting and inventory tracking.
Quantity confirmed available for fulfillment after ATP (Available to Promise) check.
Date and time when the outbound order line record was first created in the system.
Customer's purchase order line number for cross-reference and reconciliation.
Indicates whether this line is part of a direct store delivery order bypassing retailer distribution centers.
EDI transaction line reference number for electronic order processing and ASN generation.
Product expiration date for FEFO inventory rotation and shelf-life management.
Global trade item number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the product being shipped.
Serial shipping container code (SSCC) or handling unit identifier for the container holding this line item.
Indicates whether this line contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.
Sequential line number within the outbound order for ordering and identification.

Current fulfillment status of the order line in the warehouse execution lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|cancelled|short_shipped — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Total volume of the shipped quantity for this line in cubic meters.
Total weight of the shipped quantity for this line in kilograms.
Manufacturing lot or batch number for traceability and quality control.
Quantity of the SKU originally requested by the customer in the order.
Line-level OTIF performance status indicating whether the line was delivered on time and in full quantity.
Date and time when the line item was packed into shipping containers.
Quantity packed into shipping containers and ready for dispatch.
Pallet identifier if the line item was shipped on a palletized load.
Date and time when the line item was picked from warehouse inventory.
Warehouse pick zone designation for labor management and routing optimization.
Quantity physically picked from warehouse storage locations during fulfillment.
Customer-requested or system-calculated target ship date for this line.
Comma-separated list of serial numbers for serialized inventory items shipped on this line.
Date and time when the line item was shipped from the distribution center.
Quantity actually shipped to the customer, may differ from ordered due to short-ship scenarios.
Indicates whether this line was short-shipped (shipped quantity less than ordered quantity).
Reason code for short shipment (OOS=Out of Stock, DAMAGE=Damaged Inventory, RECALL=Product Recall, EXPIRED=Expired Product, ALLOCATION=Allocation Constraint).
Specific storage location or bin within the warehouse where the product was picked from.
Indicates whether this line requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation.

Unit of measure for quantities (EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, BX=Box, KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, LT=Liter, GL=Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|BX|KG|LB|LT|GL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Volume per unit of the SKU in cubic meters for space utilization and load planning.
Weight per unit of the SKU in kilograms for freight calculation and capacity planning.
Universal product code (UPC-A) for retail scanning and point-of-sale identification.
Distribution center or warehouse location code from which this line was fulfilled.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Target Ship Date
description: Target ship date for OTIF performance measurement and customer service level compliance.
Timestamp when the packing task was completed and sealed for dispatch.
Total time in minutes taken to complete the packing task, used for labor productivity analysis.
Timestamp when the packing task was initiated by the operator.
Identifier of the physical pack station or workstation where packing occurred.

Unique identifier for the pick task record. Primary key for the pick_task data product.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pick task labor costs are charged to a cost center; required for labor cost analysis and OTIF performance metrics.
Reference to the target location where picked items are staged or packed (e.g., pack station, staging lane, loading dock).
Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse where the pick task is executed.
Reference to the specific lot or batch of inventory being picked. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation compliance.
Reference to the parent outbound order or shipment order that generated this pick task.
Reference to the warehouse storage location (bin, shelf, pallet position) from which inventory is picked.
Reference to the warehouse operator or picker assigned to execute this task.
Reference to the product SKU being picked or packed in this task.
Unique identifier for the carton or shipping container into which items are packed. May be a license plate number (LPN) or SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code).
Timestamp when the pick task record was first created in the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Boolean indicator of whether this pick task is part of a Direct Store Delivery (DSD) execution workflow (true = DSD, false = standard distribution).
Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during task execution (e.g., short pick, damaged goods, location discrepancy). Null if no exception.
Free-text notes entered by the operator or supervisor describing the exception or issue encountered during task execution.
Total gross weight of the packed carton or pallet including product and packaging materials, measured in kilograms.
The GS1-128 barcode label data applied to the carton or pallet, encoding SSCC, GTIN, lot, expiry, and other supply chain attributes.
Height dimension of the packed carton or pallet, measured in centimeters.
Timestamp when the pick task record was last updated in the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Length dimension of the packed carton or pallet, measured in centimeters.
Net weight of the product content only (excluding packaging), measured in kilograms.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Eligible Flag
description: Boolean indicator of whether this pick task is subject to OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement (true = OTIF tracked, false = not tracked).
Reference to the packing station where the packing operation is performed. Applicable when task_type includes packing.
Code identifying the type of packaging material used (e.g., corrugated box, poly bag, shrink wrap). Used for cartonization and sustainability tracking.
Unique identifier for the pallet onto which cartons or items are loaded. Typically an SSCC or internal LPN.
Boolean indicator of whether the pick was accurate (true = picked quantity matches requested quantity and correct SKU/lot; false = discrepancy detected).
Human-readable pick list reference number assigned by the WMS for operator identification and tracking.
The quantity of SKU units to be picked for this task, measured in the SKU's base unit of measure.
The actual quantity of SKU units picked by the operator. May differ from pick_quantity due to short picks or overages.
The picking methodology applied: discrete (single order), batch (multiple orders), zone (by warehouse zone), wave (grouped by wave), cluster (multi-order cart picking).
Priority classification of the pick task for sequencing and resource allocation: urgent (immediate), high (expedited), normal (standard), low (backlog).
18-digit GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code uniquely identifying the logistics unit (carton or pallet) for tracking through the supply chain.
Timestamp when the pick task was assigned to an operator by the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Timestamp when the pick task was completed and confirmed by the operator or WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Total elapsed time in seconds from task start to completion. Used for labor productivity analysis and standard time calculation.
Timestamp when the operator began executing the pick task (scanned start or confirmed task initiation). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Current lifecycle status of the pick task: pending (awaiting assignment), assigned (allocated to operator), in_progress (actively being executed), completed (finished successfully), cancelled (voided), on_hold (temporarily suspended).
Discriminator indicating the type of fulfillment task: pick (picking only), pack (packing only), pick_and_pack (combined operation), or replenishment_pick (internal stock movement).
The unit of measure for pick and picked quantities (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, PL for pallet).
Reference to the wave batch that groups multiple pick tasks for optimized execution. Supports wave picking strategy.

Unique identifier for the cycle count event record. Primary key for the distribution cycle count entity.
Identifier of the supervisor or manager who approved the cycle count adjustment.
Identifier of the distribution center where the cycle count was performed.
FK to inventory cycle count
Identifier of the specific storage location (bin, rack, aisle) within the DC where the count was performed.
Identifier of the specific lot or batch being counted, critical for FEFO/FIFO inventory rotation and traceability.
Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed the physical count.
Identifier of the product SKU being counted in this cycle count line.
ABC inventory classification of the SKU at the time of count: A (high value/velocity), B (medium), C (low), D (obsolete/slow-moving).
Indicates whether the inventory adjustment has been approved by a supervisor or manager.
Indicates whether an inventory adjustment is required based on the variance exceeding tolerance thresholds.
The date and time when the cycle count adjustment was approved by a supervisor or manager.
The date and time when the physical counting activity was completed.
The method used to perform the physical count: manual (paper-based), RF scanner (handheld device), voice picking (voice-directed), or automated (RFID/sensor).
Business identifier for the cycle count event, typically generated by the WMS. Format: CC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.
The business reason triggering this cycle count: routine (scheduled ABC cycle), discrepancy (prior variance), audit (compliance), quality (QC hold), recall (product recall), transfer (location move).
The date and time when the physical counting activity began.
Current lifecycle status of the cycle count event.

Classification of the cycle count event: scheduled (routine ABC cycle), ad-hoc (unplanned), exception (triggered by discrepancy), blind (counter does not see system quantity), or targeted (specific SKU/lot focus).
The actual physical quantity counted by the warehouse associate during the cycle count event.
The date and time when this cycle count record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the inventory value variance amount.
The expiration or best-before date of the lot/batch being counted, critical for FEFO inventory rotation.
The financial impact of the variance, calculated as variance_quantity multiplied by the standard cost of the SKU. Expressed in the DC's functional currency.
The date and time when this cycle count record was last updated in the system.
Sequential number indicating which recount iteration this represents (1 for first recount, 2 for second, etc.). Null if no recount performed.
Indicates whether a recount is required due to significant variance or quality control procedures.
The date on which the cycle count was scheduled to be performed.
The inventory status of the counted stock: available (unrestricted), hold (QC hold), quarantine (quality review), damaged, expired, or reserved (allocated to orders).
The quantity of the SKU/lot recorded in the WMS at the time of the cycle count.
The acceptable variance percentage threshold. Variances within this percentage may not require adjustment.
The acceptable variance quantity threshold defined for this SKU/location combination. Variances within this threshold may not require adjustment.

The unit in which quantities are expressed: EA (each), CS (case), PL (pallet), LB (pound), KG (kilogram), L (liter), GAL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|LB|KG|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Free-text notes providing additional context or explanation for the variance observed during the cycle count.
The variance expressed as a percentage of the system quantity, calculated as (variance_quantity / system_quantity) * 100.
The difference between counted quantity and system quantity (counted minus system). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.

Root cause classification for the variance: picking error, receiving error, damage, theft, system error, location error, expiry, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: picking_error|receiving_error|damage|theft|system_error|location_error|expiry|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The unique transaction identifier generated by the WMS for this cycle count event, used for audit trail and system reconciliation.

business_glossary_term: On Time In Full (OTIF) Event ID
description: Unique identifier for the OTIF performance measurement event. Primary key for the OTIF event record.
Reference to the transportation carrier responsible for delivery. Used for carrier performance scorecarding and accountability analysis.
Reference to the delivery execution record for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) or carrier delivery. Links to the actual delivery event.
Reference to the distribution center that originated the shipment. Used for DC-level OTIF performance accountability and scorecard reporting.
Reference to the outbound shipment being measured for OTIF performance. Links to the shipment transaction that this OTIF event evaluates.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Enables OTIF Incident Management report to identify the employee responsible for resolving each OTIF event, meeting internal SLA tracking.
Reference to the specific retail store location receiving the delivery. Applicable for Direct Store Delivery (DSD) and store-level OTIF tracking.
Reference to the customer trade account receiving the shipment. Used for customer-specific OTIF scorecard and retailer compliance reporting.
The actual date the shipment was delivered to the customer location. Used to calculate on-time performance variance.
The precise timestamp when delivery was completed and proof of delivery was captured. Used for time-window OTIF compliance.
The delivery date committed to the customer per the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or purchase order terms. Baseline for on-time performance measurement.
The precise delivery date and time committed to the customer, including time window for time-sensitive deliveries. Used for precise OTIF measurement when time windows apply.
The total quantity committed to the customer per the sales order or purchase order. Baseline for in-full performance measurement. Measured in order unit of measure.
The timestamp when this OTIF event record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and record creation tracking.
The actual quantity delivered to the customer. Used to calculate in-full performance and quantity variance. Measured in order unit of measure.

The logistics channel used for delivery execution. Values include Direct Store Delivery (DSD), Less Than Truckload (LTL) carrier, Full Truckload (FTL) carrier, parcel, customer pickup, or third-party logistics (3PL).
The number of days variance between committed and actual delivery date. Negative values indicate early delivery, positive values indicate late delivery, zero indicates on-time.
Indicates whether the OTIF measurement is under dispute by the customer or internal stakeholders. True indicates active dispute, False indicates accepted measurement.
Explanation of why the OTIF measurement is disputed. Captures customer or internal objections to the performance assessment.
The current lifecycle status of the OTIF event record. Tracks progression from initial measurement through dispute resolution and final closure.

The primary root cause category for OTIF failures. Used for operational improvement analysis and accountability assignment. Values include DC pick error, inventory shortage, dock delay, carrier issue, customer refused, weather delay, system error, quality hold, damaged product, address issue, or no failure for successful deliveries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dc_pick_error|inventory_shortage|dock_delay|carrier_issue|customer_refused|weather_delay|system_error|quality_hold|damaged_product|address_issue|no_failure — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Detailed explanation of the root cause for OTIF failure. Free-text field capturing specific circumstances, corrective actions, and accountability notes.
Boolean indicator of whether the full committed quantity was delivered. True indicates complete delivery, False indicates short shipment or over shipment outside tolerance.
The acceptable variance percentage per customer SLA or business policy. Deliveries within this tolerance are considered in-full compliant.
The timestamp when this OTIF event record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
The date when the OTIF performance was measured and recorded. Used for reporting period assignment and trend analysis.
Boolean indicator of whether the shipment was delivered on or before the committed delivery date. True indicates on-time delivery, False indicates late delivery.
The customer purchase order or sales order number associated with this shipment. Business identifier for traceability to the originating order.
Composite OTIF performance indicator. True only when both on_time_flag and in_full_flag are True. Primary KPI for DC operations accountability and retailer scorecard compliance.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the retailer penalty amount. Standard currency codes such as USD, EUR, GBP.

business_glossary_term: Proof of Delivery (POD) Signature Captured Flag
description: Indicates whether an electronic or physical signature was captured at delivery. True indicates signature obtained, False indicates delivery without signature capture.
The timestamp when proof of delivery was captured, typically via electronic signature, barcode scan, or delivery confirmation. Source of truth for actual delivery time.
The unit of measure for committed and delivered quantities. Common values include cases, pallets, eaches, or other standard units.
The difference between delivered quantity and committed quantity. Negative values indicate short shipment, positive values indicate over shipment.
The percentage variance between delivered and committed quantity, calculated as (delivered - committed) / committed * 100. Used to assess severity of in-full failures.
The organizational function or external party accountable for the OTIF failure. Used for performance accountability and continuous improvement initiatives.
The financial penalty or chargeback amount assessed by the retailer for OTIF non-compliance per the customer contract or retailer compliance program. Zero for compliant deliveries.
The warehouse management system shipment number or bill of lading number. External business identifier for the shipment.
The service level tier committed to the customer. Defines the delivery speed and OTIF performance expectations per the customer agreement.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Facility manager required for safety compliance, production reporting, and labor accountability; obvious to a consumer‑goods plant manager.

Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Required for Supply Network Planning report to map each plant to its network node, enabling ATP, capacity, and logistics calculations; domain experts expect this mapping.

business_glossary_term: Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Establishment ID

Date when the facility received its current GMP certification.

Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification.
Date of the most recent quality or compliance audit conducted at the facility.
Timestamp when the manufacturing facility record was last updated in the system.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility location in decimal degrees for mapping and logistics optimization.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility location in decimal degrees for mapping and logistics optimization.
Indicates whether the facility is integrated with Siemens Opcenter MES for real-time production tracking and shop floor data collection.
Scheduled date for the next quality or compliance audit at the facility.
Total count of active production lines within the facility available for manufacturing operations.
Current operational state of the manufacturing facility in its lifecycle.

business_glossary_term: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Establishment Number
description: OSHA establishment identifier for workplace safety reporting and compliance tracking.
Legal ownership structure of the facility: owned (company-owned), leased (long-term lease), contract (third-party operated), joint_venture (shared ownership).
Primary contact phone number for the manufacturing facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Unique business identifier for the manufacturing plant used across SAP S/4HANA PP module and operational systems. Typically 4-10 alphanumeric characters.
Postal or ZIP code for the facility address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Main product category or line manufactured at this facility (e.g., skincare, haircare, household cleaning, personal care).
Maximum daily production capacity of the facility measured in finished goods units per day under normal operating conditions.
Standard operating shift pattern for the facility: single (8 hours), two_shift (16 hours), three_shift (24 hours), continuous (24/7), flexible (variable).
Total floor area of the manufacturing facility in square feet, including production, warehouse, and administrative spaces.
State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located.
Internal sustainability performance rating for the facility based on environmental impact, energy efficiency, waste reduction, and sustainable sourcing practices.
IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin) used for production scheduling and shift planning.
Annual water consumption of the facility measured in cubic meters, tracked for environmental compliance and sustainability reporting.
Total number of employees (full-time equivalent) assigned to the manufacturing facility.
Unique identifier for the production line. Primary key for the production line master record.
FK to finance.cost_center
Foreign key linking to sustainability.energy_certificate. Business justification: Energy certificates certify renewable energy for a line; linking enables line‑level energy compliance tracking.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Production line belongs to a specific manufacturing facility; replace generic facility reference with direct link to manufacturing_facility.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Line supervisor drives OEE, quality and shift planning; plant experts expect a dedicated supervisor link per line.
Indicates whether this production line handles products containing allergens. Critical for cross-contamination prevention and regulatory compliance.
Comma-separated list of allergen types handled on this line (e.g., nuts, dairy, gluten). Used for allergen management and cleaning validation protocols.
Physical asset tag number assigned to the production line for asset management and tracking purposes. Links to the fixed asset register in the ERP system.
Degree of automation on the production line. Indicates the level of human intervention required and impacts labor planning and quality consistency.
Standard time in minutes required to change over the production line from one product or batch to another. Used for production scheduling and OEE loss calculation.
Reference to the cleaning validation protocol document applicable to this production line. Ensures GMP compliance and prevents cross-contamination.
Date when the production line was commissioned and became operational. Used for asset lifecycle tracking and depreciation calculations.
Timestamp when this production line record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Standard number of operators required to run the production line at full capacity. Used for labor planning and cost allocation.
Accounting depreciation method applied to this production line asset. Used for financial reporting and asset valuation.
Theoretical maximum production capacity of the line measured in units per hour under ideal conditions. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.
Average energy consumption per unit produced on this line, measured in kilowatt-hours. Key metric for sustainability reporting and cost analysis.
Indicates whether the production line requires controlled environmental conditions such as temperature, humidity, or air quality. Critical for GMP compliance and product quality.

GMP classification level of the production line indicating the quality and cleanliness standards required for products manufactured on this line. Critical for regulatory compliance and product safety.
Acceptable relative humidity range for the production line as a percentage. Format: min-max (e.g., 40-60). Used for environmental monitoring and compliance.
Total installed electrical power capacity of the production line in kilowatts. Used for energy consumption tracking and sustainability reporting.
Date of the most recent major overhaul or refurbishment of the production line. Used for maintenance planning and asset condition assessment.
Timestamp when this production line record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Business identifier code for the production line. Unique within the facility and used for operational reference in MES systems and production scheduling.
Physical length of the production line in meters. Used for facility layout planning and material flow analysis.
Human-readable name of the production line. Used for display in dashboards, reports, and operator interfaces.
Classification of the production line based on its primary manufacturing function. Determines the type of operations and products that can be processed on this line.
Average time in hours between equipment failures on this production line. Key reliability metric used for maintenance optimization and capacity planning.
Average time in hours required to repair and restore the production line to operational status after a failure. Used for downtime analysis and maintenance resource planning.
Unique asset identifier for this production line in the Siemens Opcenter MES system. Used for real-time data collection, production tracking, and integration with shop floor systems.
User identifier of the person who last modified this production line record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Date of the next planned preventive maintenance activity for this production line. Used for maintenance scheduling and production planning coordination.

business_glossary_term: Nominal Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) Target Percent
description: Target OEE percentage for this production line. OEE is calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality and represents the benchmark for line performance evaluation.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational considerations specific to this production line.
Current operational status of the production line. Indicates whether the line is available for production scheduling and execution.
Type and model of the PLC system controlling the production line. Used for technical support, integration planning, and system upgrade management.
Comma-separated list of product categories that can be manufactured on this line. Used for production planning and product-line matching in scheduling systems.
Standard frequency for quality control inspections on this production line. Defines the QC sampling plan and compliance with GMP requirements.
Source system from which this production line record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PP, Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control And Data Acquisition (SCADA) Integration Enabled
description: Indicates whether the production line is integrated with the SCADA system for real-time monitoring and control. Enables remote visibility and data collection.
Target scrap rate percentage for this production line. Used for quality performance benchmarking and waste reduction initiatives.
Standard operating shift pattern for this production line. Defines the daily operating schedule and impacts capacity calculations.
Acceptable operating temperature range for the production line in Celsius. Format: min-max (e.g., 18-25). Used for environmental monitoring and compliance.
Expected useful life of the production line in years for depreciation and asset lifecycle planning purposes.
Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Labor and overhead budgeting links each work center to a finance cost center for expense tracking.
Reference to the employee responsible for managing and maintaining this work center, supporting accountability and contact management.
Reference to the manufacturing plant where this work center is physically located.
Reference to the production line to which this work center belongs, enabling line-level capacity and performance analysis.
Total available production hours per day for this work center after accounting for planned downtime, breaks, and maintenance windows.
Next scheduled calibration date for measurement and control instruments on this work center, ensuring accuracy and compliance with quality standards.
Unit of measure for the rated capacity indicating how production output is quantified for this work center.

Date when the work center was first commissioned and put into production service, establishing the asset lifecycle baseline.
Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.
Standard number of operators required to run this work center during normal production operations, used for labor planning and scheduling.
Date when the work center was retired from production service, supporting asset lifecycle tracking and capacity planning.
Average energy consumption in kilowatt-hours per operating hour, supporting sustainability reporting and cost allocation per ISO 14001 environmental management.

Current qualification status of the work center per GMP requirements. IQ (Installation Qualification), OQ (Operational Qualification), PQ (Performance Qualification) track equipment validation stages per ISO 22716.
